a11yDesktopPlatformNote: Desktop app: everything above plus high-quality local voice, Sage microphone, and Sage reply read-aloud (download only after you accept).

a11yPiperLocaleHint: Piper voice language (also used for Sage replies).
a11yPiperVoiceHint: Higher-quality neural voice (~20 MB download, once per language). If off, lessons and labels use system speech.

a11ySageAutoSpeakHint: Also toggled from the speaker button in Sage chat.

metaphorText: Like a tree, knowledge is nourished by the community (roots) to expand its branches and offer leaves of wisdom (lessons) to anyone who wishes to explore them.
noTreesLead: Add a public or private tree, or plant a new local garden to start exploring.
pdfDisclaimerText:
This document was automatically generated by Arborito, a decentralized knowledge explorer.
Tree: {treeName}
Author: {author}
Source: {source}

The content, text, images, and intellectual property contained herein belong exclusively to the authors and maintainers of the source listed. Arborito (Treesys) acts solely as a visualization engine and assumes no responsibility for the accuracy, legality, copyright status, or safety of the material presented in this document. Users are responsible for verifying the source of the information.
pdfPopupBlocked: Pop-up blocked. Allow pop-ups for PDF generation.
privateTreesLocalMissing: Could not find that local tree on this device.
privateTreesOnlyLocalSource: Only a local tree can be privately synced. Open one from My trees first.
privateTreesPublishCta: Sync encrypted draft to your account (other devices)
privateTreesPublishedOk: Draft synced to your account (still private).
privateTreesStopSync: Stop syncing this tree to your account
privateTreesStopSyncBody: Other devices you sign in on will no longer see this tree. The local copy on this device stays.
saveFailedLocal: Local save failed. Node not found.
sourceManagerDesc: Your branches, playlists and courses from the network.
treeLoadSlowHint: Still loading tree from the network…
moreMenuRowForumSub: Topics and posts by course area
searchBookmarksEmpty: Bookmark a section with the star in the lesson toolbar to see it here.
searchIndexBuilding: Preparing search on this device… (a few seconds; you can keep reading the map)
searchIndexFailed: Search could not finish preparing on this device. You can keep using the course—try closing search and opening it again later. If it keeps happening, try another browser or reload the page.
tourSourcesPickerBody: Pick a branch from this list (local or on the network). Once it loads, the tour continues on the map at the tree root.

uiTourSteps.title: Tree root
uiTourSteps.body: This is where the curriculum starts 🌳. From the root you open modules 🗂️, lessons 📄, and exams ⚔️. Tap a node to enter; the panel below lists its children.

uiTourSteps.title: Back to the map
uiTourSteps.body: The logo returns you to the tree home when you want a clean overview.

uiTourSteps.title: Switch tree
uiTourSteps.body: Come back here to load another curriculum or library when you want a different course.

uiTourSteps.title: Arcade
uiTourSteps.body: Play friendly practice games that can adapt to what you are studying.

uiTourSteps.title: Construction Mode
uiTourSteps.body: Turn this on to add folders and lessons, or reorganize editable trees.

uiTourSteps.title: Search topics
uiTourSteps.body: Open search to jump to a topic by name without walking every branch.

uiTourSteps.title: Profile & backpack
uiTourSteps.body: Your avatar opens the menu: profile, export progress, achievements, and optional cross-device sync.

uiTourSteps.title: Guide in Sage
uiTourSteps.body: Tap the owl 🦉 and choose “What is Arborito?” for the topic guide: navigation, garden, arcade, and more.

uiTourStepsConstruction.title: You are in construction mode
uiTourStepsConstruction.body: The helmet ⛑️ is active: you can now edit the tree. Tap any node on the map to open its edit panel on the right. Press this button again to return to study mode.

uiTourStepsConstruction.title: Edit history
uiTourStepsConstruction.body: The 🕒 clock opens the timeline of your edits: step backward and forward between versions without losing your place, and compare before/after.

uiTourStepsConstruction.title: Content language
uiTourStepsConstruction.body: 🌐 Switch or add the content language for the tree you are editing. Each language has its own titles and copy; you can keep several in parallel.

uiTourStepsConstruction.title: About this tree — fill in before publishing
uiTourStepsConstruction.body: ℹ️ You need to fill this card before publishing: description (min. 5 characters) and an optional support link. Save with the button. To appear as the creator, sign in with your online account.

uiTourStepsConstruction.title: Collaborators & permissions
uiTourStepsConstruction.body: Decide who can edit this tree: invite collaborators, tune roles (editors, proposers), and review the history of permission changes.

uiTourStepsConstruction.title: Publish the tree
uiTourStepsConstruction.body: When the draft is ready, publish online or update the public copy you already shared. Your local draft keeps editing on its own without touching the public version.

uiTourStepsConstruction.title: Optional backup
uiTourStepsConstruction.body: Your changes live on this device first. If you want, enable sync with your account so you have a copy across your devices.

uiTourStepsConstruction.title: Your profile
uiTourStepsConstruction.body: Your avatar opens your profile: name, picture, and the optional account. If you have an account and publish, the tree is linked to that profile; if you don't, you can still create — everything stays on this device.

uiTourStepsConstruction.title: If you get stuck, the owl 🦉
uiTourStepsConstruction.body: If anything is unclear, come back here: Sage walks you through construction step by step with contextual hints based on what you are doing.

uiTourStepsConstructionMobile.title: You are in construction mode
uiTourStepsConstructionMobile.body: The ← arrow in the dock returns to study mode when you are done editing. While you are here, tap any node on the map to open its edit panel.

uiTourStepsConstructionMobile.title: Edit history
uiTourStepsConstructionMobile.body: History 🕒: review earlier steps and undo or redo changes to the map structure without losing your place.

uiTourStepsConstructionMobile.title: About this tree — fill in before publishing
uiTourStepsConstructionMobile.body: ℹ️ You need to fill this card before publishing: description (min. 5 characters) and an optional support link. Save with the button. To appear as the creator, sign in with your online account.

uiTourStepsConstructionMobile.title: Language & more tools
uiTourStepsConstructionMobile.body: 🌐 Switch or add curriculum languages, reach permissions and collaborators, compare draft vs public, and other course actions.

uiTourStepsConstructionMobile.title: Publish the tree
uiTourStepsConstructionMobile.body: Publish the tree or push updates to the online copy from here. Your local draft remains separate.

uiTourStepsConstructionMobile.title: Your profile
uiTourStepsConstructionMobile.body: Your avatar opens your profile: name, picture, and the optional account. If you have an account and publish, the tree is linked to that profile; if you don't, you can still create — everything stays on this device.

uiTourStepsConstructionMobile.title: If you get stuck, the owl 🦉
uiTourStepsConstructionMobile.body: If anything is unclear, come back here: Sage walks you through construction step by step with contextual hints based on what you are doing.

uiTourStepsMobile.title: Tree root
uiTourStepsMobile.body: This circle with the logo is the curriculum root 🌳. Tap the nodes below to enter modules and lessons.

uiTourStepsMobile.title: Home
uiTourStepsMobile.body: Return to the main tree view from anywhere.

uiTourStepsMobile.title: Search
uiTourStepsMobile.body: Find lessons by name without crossing the whole map.

uiTourStepsMobile.title: Sage — your guide
uiTourStepsMobile.body: The owl opens shortcuts and “What is Arborito?”. No required AI — only what is in your course.

uiTourStepsMobile.title: Arcade
uiTourStepsMobile.body: Practice games tied to what you are learning.

uiTourStepsMobile.title: More
uiTourStepsMobile.body: Handbook, trees, achievements, language, and tools. Profile and theme sit at the top right.

uiTourStepsLessonEdit.title: Lesson card — fill in before publishing
uiTourStepsLessonEdit.body: Set the lesson title, description and emoji. These show up in the tree map, in search and in Arcade games. Lessons without these stay as drafts and won't be published.

uiTourStepsLessonEdit.title: Lesson editor toolbar
uiTourStepsLessonEdit.body: Format text, undo, and — most importantly — insert blocks: callouts, images, videos, mini-games and the all-in-one questionnaire. Tap Save when done; drafts stay local until you publish.

uiTourStepsLessonEdit.title: Lesson questionnaire
uiTourStepsLessonEdit.body: Insert a questionnaire from the editor toolbar. With topic, definition, and correct answer you unlock Recall. Add cloze words, wrong answers, or ordered steps to unlock more Arcade modes.

uiTourStepsLessonEdit.title: Syllabus & Arcade status
uiTourStepsLessonEdit.body: Open the syllabus with the list button. Each section shows 🎮 when it has a questionnaire: grey = incomplete, green = ready for static Arcade games (no AI).

uiTourStepsLessonEdit.title: Save your work
uiTourStepsLessonEdit.body: Saves the lesson into your local draft of the tree. Use the global Publish button later to push the changes online.

uiTourStepsSourcesPicker.title: Tabs: Branches and Trees
uiTourStepsSourcesPicker.body: Two separate tabs: Branches are full courses (.arborito). Trees are playlists you build by combining branches — remix, publish, and share one link.

uiTourStepsSourcesPicker.title: Branches tab
uiTourStepsSourcesPicker.body: Your courses live here: plant, import, or install from the network. Open one to study or edit.

uiTourStepsSourcesPicker.title: Pick your first branch
uiTourStepsSourcesPicker.body: Search or tap a branch card. When it loads, the tour continues on the map at the root.

uiTourStepsSourcesPicker.title: Trees tab
uiTourStepsSourcesPicker.body: Combine branches into playlists: remix, publish to the network, and share one link.

uiTourStepsSourcesPicker.title: Your trees
uiTourStepsSourcesPicker.body: Search your playlists, open one, or tap Create tree. After publishing, use Share to copy a link anyone can open.

sageAiOptionalBanner: Optional. Arborito works fully without AI — turn it on only if you want a hand.
sageAiOptionalBannerShort: AI is optional — lessons, quizzes, and games work without it.
sageAiThirdPartyLicenses: Third-party license ({model} and any other you pick): see the Hugging Face model card.
sageBrowserModelHint: Hugging Face GGUF model (desktop app only). Downloaded once, runs locally via native llama.cpp.
sageBrowserModelPublicHint: Public Hugging Face GGUF models need no auth. On the web, use Expert mode with Ollama/OpenAI.
sageConfigDesc: These settings tune the experimental AI model the owl uses. AI is optional and runs on your device only.
sageContextPresetHint: Model window: Micro 4k · Minimal 6k · Balanced 8k. Auto-tuned to your hardware on first run.
sageContextStrictHint: On by default: Sage answers only from your lesson context. If it is not there, it says it does not know. Turn off to allow general knowledge (may hallucinate).
sageDynamicHello: Experimental mode: ask about any topic in your tree. I use lesson content only.
sageExperimentalBuggyWarn: Heads-up: this preview can be slow or wrong. Guide mode (no AI) is still the recommended path.
sageExperimentalChatNote: Experimental AI is local: it processes your tree on-device without sending lessons to servers. You can switch back to Guide mode anytime.
sageExperimentalDisclaimer: Opt-in only — never turns itself on. The model runs on your device; nothing you type goes to any cloud service.

sageExpertApiHint: Examples: Ollama at http://127.0.0.1:11434/v1, OpenAI at https://api.openai.com/v1. Keys stay in your browser localStorage only.
sageExpertApiKeyLabel: API key (optional, stored on this device only)
sageExpertApiPrivacyNote: When expert mode is on, your questions and lesson context are sent to the API you configure. Review that provider's privacy policy.
sageExpertSettingsDesc: Connect Ollama, OpenAI, or another compatible API. No heavy downloads in the browser.
sageGamesAiSettingsNote: Optional. Runs on your own device. Guide mode never uses AI.
sageGdprConnectsNote: Everything runs on your device; no account needed.
sageGdprText: The {model} model (~{mb} MB) will download once from Hugging Face.

sageGuideSageIntro: Hi — I'm **Sage**, your guide inside Arborito. Tap any card below to find your way through the course or learn how the app works.
sageGuideSageIntroCon: **Construction mode.** I'll walk you through adding lessons, editing nodes and publishing your tree when it's ready.
sageGuideSageIntroLesson: You're inside a lesson. Tap a card below and I'll tell you **where you are**, **how to continue**, or whatever you need.
sageGuideTipEmpty:

sageGuideTreeEmpty: Explore the map to discover content.
sageHello: Hoot hoot! I am awake. Ask me anything.

sageModeGuideTooltip: No AI: only text the author already wrote in the lesson.
sageQuickQuiz: Ask me a practice question from this lesson.
sageQuizEmpty:
This lesson doesn't have a questionnaire yet.

• That's normal — not every lesson includes one.
• When one is added, you'll be able to review it from the **Arcade** with minigames built from its questions.
sageQuizStatusHint: Heads up: I don't show answers here, to avoid spoilers. The review is solved on the lesson page itself.
sageQuizStatusIncomplete:
**The review is started but incomplete.**

• The author left some fields unfinished, so a few questions or hints may be missing.
• You can still scroll down on the lesson and practice what's there.
sageQuizStatusNone:
**This lesson has no review.**

• When you finish reading, head back to the map with **🏠** and pick another lesson.
• If you want to drill the whole tree, open the **Arcade** from the dock.
sageQuizStatusReady:
**The review is ready for you to solve.**

• Scroll down on the lesson page to the review section.
• Answer it right there — your progress is saved locally on this device.
• If you'd rather drill with minigames, open **«Practice in the Arcade»** from this panel.
sageSectionQuizIncomplete: There's a review started, but it's not complete yet.
sageSettingsBrowserDesc: On the web, only expert mode with an external API is available.
sageSettingsDesktopNativeDesc: Native multi-threaded llama.cpp. One-time GGUF download; much faster than any in-browser AI.
sageSettingsModelHint: Format user/repo:file.gguf — downloaded when you use Sage.
sageSupportBackMap:
**Back to the map** takes you to the course's tree.

• Tap **🏠** in the bottom dock or the Arborito logo at the top.
• You can also close this panel with the **×** or by swiping down.
• Once on the map, tap any circle to open another lesson.
sageSupportContinue:
You're on **«{name}»**. A few ideas for what to do next:

• If this lesson has a **review at the bottom**, finish it before moving on — it locks in what you just read.
• Want to drill with minigames? Tap **«Practice in the Arcade»** from this panel.
• To switch topic, head back to the map with **🏠** and tap another circle on the tree.
• Completing **every lesson in a module** earns you a seed 🌱 — they collect in your backpack.

Tip: there's no fixed order, so feel free to explore the topics that interest you most.
sageSupportLessonHelp:
Each **lesson** is a page on the map (the «leaf» circles). Here's how they work:

• The actual content lives on the **page**, not in this panel — I only help you find your way around.
• If there's a **review**, it sits at the bottom of the lesson. Answer it there to fix what you learned.
• Mark the lesson as **«Completed»** when you finish reading and reviewing it.
• Completing every lesson in a module earns a **seed 🌱**, which you'll see in your backpack along with your streak and XP.

If you get lost, tap **«Where am I?»** above in this panel and I'll show you where you are in the tree.
sageSupportWhere:
You're reading **«{name}»**.

**Path in the tree**
{path}

To switch topic:

• Tap **🏠** in the dock to return to the map and pick another circle.
• Or open the **side index** and browse modules.
• To search by name, use **«Search»** from the menu or from Sage's panel.
sageTopicConAddB1: Tap a module on the map and press + (bottom bar on mobile).
sageTopicConAddB2: Pick module, lesson or exam and name it.
sageTopicConAddB3: You can reorder nodes by dragging afterward.
sageTopicConAddLead: Building a course is like planting: drop a module, then add leaves (lessons) or exams to it.
sageTopicConAddS1b: Tap the parent module on the map. On mobile, use the + button in the dock; on desktop, right-click to open the menu.
sageTopicConAddS2b: You can create a module (another branch), a lesson (text + optional quiz) or an exam (lesson with required quiz).
sageTopicConAddS3b: Type a clear name and save. The node appears on the map right away and is ready to edit.
sageTopicConAddS4b: Drag any node onto another branch to reorganize. The tree rearranges itself.
sageTopicConEditB1: Context menu: properties, move, duplicate or delete.
sageTopicConEditB2: Leaves open the lesson and quiz editor.
sageTopicConEditLead: Every node is edited separately. Leaves also have a rich text editor and an optional quiz that feeds the Arcade.
sageTopicConEditS1b: Tap a circle (module) or a leaf to select it. You'll see quick actions: edit, move, duplicate or delete.
sageTopicConEditS2b: Rename, change type or tweak visibility. Changes show up on the map immediately.
sageTopicConEditS3b: On a lesson, Open takes you to the rich editor: paste markdown, images and code blocks.
sageTopicConEditS4b: Questions feed the Arcade minigames. The more you add, the better your readers can review.
sageTopicConPubB1: Before publishing: fill the “About this tree” card (description, minimum 5 characters) on top of the map and save it. Without that you can't publish.
sageTopicConPubB2: Every change saves instantly on your device. Publish shares the tree with others (top or bottom bar).
sageTopicConPubB3: You can undo or revert to a saved version at any time.
sageTopicConPubLead: Publishing shares your tree with the network. First, complete the course card so it presents well.
sageTopicConPubS1b: Open “About this tree” above the map. Set the title, description (at least 5 chars) and other basic details.
sageTopicConPubS2b: Every change is saved on this device instantly. You can undo or revert to earlier versions.
sageTopicConPubS3b: Press Publish (top or bottom bar) to upload the tree to the decentralized network. It will appear in other people's directory.
sageTopicConPubS4b: Whenever you change something important, publish again. Readers receive the new version next time they open the course.
sageTopicGardenB1: Seeds 🌱 mark progress when you complete modules.
sageTopicGardenB2: Your streak rewards studying a little each day.
sageTopicGardenB3: Your backpack suggests what to review based on what you read.
sageTopicGardenLead: Arborito gamifies study without pressure: seeds, streak and smart review.
sageTopicGardenS1b: Complete modules to earn seeds. They reflect real progress in the course.
sageTopicGardenS2b: Studying a little each day keeps your streak. No marathon required.
sageTopicGardenS3b: Your backpack suggests what to review based on what you read and game results.
sageTopicIntroB1: Courses as trees: branches (topics) and leaves (lessons). You choose the path.
sageTopicIntroB2: Progress stays on this device; study at your own pace.
sageTopicIntroB3: Arcade mini-games review when a lesson has a quiz.

treeReportPolicyBody:
Your report is stored safely (reason and who sent it) so the app can weigh it. It does not delete lessons or remove the course from the internet.

If many people report the same course in a short time, it may stop appearing in the public list inside the app. The app does that on its own—no one at Treesys reads every report like a support chat.

Copyright issues use a separate legal process described in the app’s legal notices.

Normal reports do not email the distributor. For the operator, use only the options below.

aboutPrivacyTabBlurb: By default your learning data stays in this browser. The sections below explain how GDPR applies to the app, public online trees (nostr://), forum and optional network sync, optional Sage AI, and what you can do.

privacyAiBrowserLine: Optional, off by default. Only if you opt in does Arborito load the bundled AI runtime and fetch model weights from Hugging Face. After that, every answer is generated locally in your browser — nothing you type leaves your device.

privacyDeviceDataLead: Delete everything Arborito stores in this browser (progress, settings, forum drafts, optional consents), or reset only optional consents for external lesson media, in-browser AI, and inline games. Data you already published on a public network tree is not removed from the network—use the forum and construction tools for that.
privacyLegalBasisBody:
Local data (progress, settings, drafts): needed to provide the app you use (Art. 6(1)(b)).

Public network (Nostr relays, forum, nostr:// trees): only if you enable it. The basis is your explicit consent when accepting the privacy notice on the welcome screen before any WebSocket is opened (Art. 6(1)(a)). When some relays are located outside the EU/EEA (notably the United States), that same acceptance also works as the informed consent for the international transfer without an adequacy decision (Art. 49(1)(a)). We do not rely on «legitimate interest» (Art. 6(1)(f)) for sending your IP to non-EU relays: data-protection authorities reject that ground when there are no contractual safeguards with the operator, and Nostr — being a decentralised network — provides none.

In-browser AI, external media, inline games: explicit in-app consent, revocable in Profile (Art. 6(1)(a)).

Accessibility and voice (desktop): local voice download and the Sage microphone are optional; explicit consent when you turn them on (Art. 6(1)(a)). In the browser, read-aloud uses system speech with no download and no data sent to Arborito servers.

Legal obligation: only where applicable law requires it (Art. 6(1)(c)).
privacyLocalFirstCallout: Local-first: Your learning progress is stored in this browser (LocalStorage). By default it is not sent to a central Treesys database.
privacyNostrRelaysBody:
What they are. Public curricula linked with nostr:// are read and published over the Nostr network. Your browser opens secure WebSockets (wss://) to relays that act as meeting points for tree data and, where applicable, the forum.

Operators and privacy. Each relay is run by an independent third party. They may have their own legal terms and privacy notices; you should read the information published by the operator of the service you are using.

Default relays in this version. Unless you change the configuration, the browser connects to: wss://relay.tchncs.de (Germany, EU), wss://nostr.einundzwanzig.space (Germany, EU), wss://purplepag.es (multi-region, profile-specialised), wss://nos.lol (United States), and wss://relay.primal.net (United States). Each connection exposes your IP address to the relay operator so messages can be routed.

International transfers (GDPR Art. 49(1)(a)). Some relays operate outside the EU/EEA (notably in the United States), where the level of personal-data protection is not equivalent to the GDPR and we cannot sign Standard Contractual Clauses with each operator (Nostr is a decentralised network with no contract between client and relay). Before opening any WebSocket we therefore ask for your explicit, informed consent on the welcome screen (the «Accept» button). That consent is the specific legal basis under Article 49(1)(a) GDPR for occasional transfers to third countries without an adequacy decision: you accept that your IP and connection time will be visible to those operators in exchange for using the public features (forum, shared trees, ranking). If you do not accept, Arborito does not open any relay connection — the local-first mode keeps working without the network.

Withdrawing consent. You can withdraw your consent at any time from Profile → Privacy → «Reset optional consents». From that moment we will not open new connections; data that already left the browser cannot be retrieved from the Nostr network (no central operator controls it).

Changing relays. You can replace the default list from the advanced configuration (see docs/NOSTR_RELAYS_CONFIGURATION.md in the repository). For instance, you can keep an EU-only set if your organisation requires it.

Already published content. Changing how your browser connects does not by itself erase information that was already spread on the network; availability or speed may vary depending on the network and operators.
privacyNetworkConsentGrantedBody: You accepted the privacy policy. Arborito can reach Nostr relays and the WebTorrent network on this device.
privacyNetworkConsentMissingBody: You have not accepted (or you withdrew) the privacy policy. Arborito will NOT contact any Nostr relay or WebTorrent peer until you accept.
privacyResetConsentConfirmBody: We will clear saved choices for external lesson media, in-browser AI (Sage), local voice and microphone (desktop), the inline-game warning AND the network consent (Nostr / WebTorrent). Your learning progress and backups are not deleted, but Arborito will stop connecting to external networks until you accept again.
privacySecretsBody:
What is stored. Your Arborito account secrets — the per-user Nostr signing keys and your account sync code — are stored in this browser's localStorage, on this device, in plain text, alongside Arborito's other settings. There is no server backup and we never see them.

What this means in practice. Anyone with physical or remote access to this browser profile can copy them. Browser extensions you install have access to localStorage. Sharing a device without a per-user OS account is risky. Clearing site data, uninstalling the browser, or using "Wipe local data" deletes them permanently — there is no "forgot password" recovery.

What we recommend. If your tree carries identity value (signed publications, collaborator role, certificates), back up your account sync code out-of-band (paper, password manager) before relying on it. Treat these secrets like a private SSH key, not like a website login.

Your rights. Because these secrets never leave the device, there is no central database to query, rectify, or delete (GDPR Arts. 15–17 are satisfied locally). The "Wipe local data" button below is the canonical erasure mechanism for this device.

privacyText:
1. Data controller

{impressum}

2. Local storage (default)

Local-first: Arborito runs as a client-side application. By default, your progress, settings, and related data are stored locally in your browser (LocalStorage). We do not operate our own central database for your personal learning data.

3. GDPR & who is responsible

If you are in the EU/EEA or UK, the GDPR applies to personal data. For the Arborito software as published by Treesys, use the contact details in the Legal tab. For content and activity in a public online tree (a nostr:// link) or a tree loaded from elsewhere, the person or organisation that publishes or moderates that tree decides what is shared and may be the main contact for questions about that content.

Treesys provides open-source software; it does not review every community tree. Use the in-app controls to delete your posts, remove your online identity for a tree, export a backup file, delete all data on this device (Profile), or clear site data in your browser.

4. Public online trees, forum & sync

When you open a public tree, your browser may connect to peer-to-peer relays. Forum messages are cryptographically signed and readable by others with the same link. Optional learning progress sync is encrypted with keys stored on your device; the network stores ciphertext, not your plaintext progress.

Deletion and moderation use signed records so the app stops showing removed content in the canonical view. Copies may still exist on other devices or caches—this is a general limit of distributed systems.

5. Third-party services

Depending on how you use the app, network requests may reach:

  • Static hosting: Your browser loads the app HTML, scripts, and styles from whatever origin you use (for example a local server or your own deployment).
  • Nostr relays (nostr:// trees): Your browser opens secure WebSockets (wss://) to relays according to the Arborito installation or version you use. Operators may process typical TLS connection metadata and publish their own legal and privacy information. Forum traffic and public curriculum data follow the Nostr protocol.
  • WebTorrent (optional): some trees and the global directory may load over P2P. Your IP becomes visible to other peers in the swarm. If you would rather not expose it, avoid trees distributed via torrent; the rest keeps working over Nostr alone.
  • bundled runtime & Hugging Face (optional): On desktop, if you enable Sage and accept the download prompt: native llama.cpp binaries and model files are fetched from Hugging Face (and related CDNs for weights only). On web, Sage chat uses your own Expert API if configured — no in-browser model download.

No marketing stack: No Google Fonts, advertising pixels, tag managers, or social embeds ship with the app shell.

6. Your rights (GDPR)

Depending on context, you may have rights under Articles 15–22 GDPR (access, rectification, erasure, restriction, portability, objection). You can delete local data with Profile → Delete all data on this device (removes Arborito storage in this browser and reloads the app), or by clearing site data for this origin in your browser settings. You can reset optional consents for external media, in-browser AI, and inline games from Profile without deleting your progress. For data you published to a public tree, use the built-in tools (delete message, remove online identity) and contact the tree publisher if you need further help. In the EU, applicable law also allows lodging a complaint with the data protection authority in your country.
privacyWebTorrentBody:
What it is. Some trees and the global directory mirror can be distributed over WebTorrent — a peer-to-peer protocol where your browser exchanges data directly with other devices ("peers") that have the same content. This is what allows large curricula to scale without a central server.

Your IP address is visible to peers. By design, every peer in the swarm sees the IP address of every other peer (it is how the connection happens). This includes peers in any country. Your IP is also briefly visible to public WebRTC trackers used to discover peers. If you do not want your IP visible to strangers, avoid loading WebTorrent-distributed trees; trees that travel over Nostr only do not expose your IP this way.

VPN / Tor. A VPN masks your IP to peers as long as it is active; Tor does not work for WebTorrent's WebRTC transport. Your provider's terms still apply.

No central log. Arborito does not run a central swarm tracker and does not collect peer data. What you see is what the protocol exposes; we cannot delete IPs from other peers' memory after the fact.

exportArchiveGuideTxt:
Arborito export (.arborito)

A ZIP file — unzip it and the folders are the course.

Two rules:
  1. Parallel lessons/ES/… and lessons/EN/… — same position (01/02) = same lesson.
  2. Text after "NN -" in each file is the lesson title.

Full author format: files/AUTORIA.md

Re-import: Arborito → Trees → Import.

publicTreePublishEventPackageBody:
The app could not build a valid message for the network. This is usually about the course data or size, not your Wi‑Fi or relays.

You can try:
• Shortening or splitting very large lessons
• Removing odd characters from pasted text (invisible symbols, broken emojis)
• Exporting a backup (.arborito), then updating Arborito and trying again

publicTreePublishForbiddenBody:
The configured relays accepted the connection but rejected your event (typical messages: “blocked”, “restricted”, “only notes signed by the owner”, “not authorized”, or “does not have permission to write”). This is not a Wi‑Fi problem — the relays simply refuse writes from your key.

What you can do:
• Open About this tree → Network health and replace the relays with public ones that accept general writes (avoid “haven” or single-owner private relays).
• If you maintain this Arborito deployment, adjust DEFAULT_NOSTR_RELAYS (docs/NOSTR_RELAYS_CONFIGURATION.md).

missionText: Arborito is the open education platform driven by Treesys, the organization behind this ecosystem. Learn at your own pace, explore a living map of ideas, and give something back: a fix, a translation, a new lesson. Education is a human right, not a business—and every honest contribution helps the forest keep growing.
